backlog.title: Bugfix-002: Silent wrong-behavior fixes (config path fail-fast, manifest output/strip_code_fence honored, UX error wording)
backlog.description: Second-pass QA bugfix for Stagehand v1.0 (post bugfix-001). Four SILENT wrong-behavior bugs (no crashes, no data-integrity regressions) centering on the config↔manifest handoff seam and user-facing error wording: (1) an explicit --config/STAGEHAND_CONFIG to a NONEXISTENT file is silently ignored so a real agent is invoked; (2) manifest-level output/strip_code_fence are clobbered by [generation] defaults (JSON mode dead at manifest level); (3) unresolved merge conflicts surface raw multi-line git write-tree stderr; (4) --dry-run exits 3/124 with a full rescue recipe. The snapshot/atomic-commit core (§13/§18.1), rescue/timeout/CAS/signal mapping, and root-commit path are verified correct and MUST NOT regress. Findings in plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/.

milestones.title: Issue 1 — Explicit --config / STAGEHAND_CONFIG to a missing file fails fast instead of silently invoking a real agent
milestones.description: Distinguish the EXPLICIT override path (--config / STAGEHAND_CONFIG) from DISCOVERY (globalConfigPath) inside config.Load. A missing explicit file must be a hard error (exit 1, naming the path) — consistent with how a malformed/directory explicit path already errors — while the discovery path continues to tolerate absence via loadTOML's (nil,nil) contract. PRD §15.2/§16.1/§13.5/§19.

tasks.title: Make config.Load treat a missing EXPLICIT config path (--config / STAGEHAND_CONFIG) as a hard error
tasks.description: In internal/config/load.go Load(), compute whether globalPath originated from an explicit source (opts.ConfigPathOverride != "" OR os.Getenv("STAGEHAND_CONFIG") != ""). When loadTOML(globalPath) returns (nil, nil) AND the source was explicit, return fmt.Errorf("config file not found: %s", globalPath). The discovery path (globalConfigPath()) keeps the existing nil-means-absent behavior. No change to loadTOML, file.go, or the --config wiring in root.go (ConfigPathOverride already flows through PersistentPreRunE).

subtasks.title: Add explicit-vs-discovery distinction in config.Load and fail fast on a missing explicit path
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/issue_analysis.md ISSUE 1. config.Load (internal/config/load.go:48-65) resolves globalPath = opts.ConfigPathOverride > os.Getenv("STAGEHAND_CONFIG") > globalConfigPath(), then calls loadTOML(globalPath). loadTOML (internal/config/file.go:99-102) returns (nil,nil) on os.IsNotExist — this contract is the DISCOVERY path's 'layer absent' sentinel and MUST be preserved. The bug: there is no record of whether globalPath was explicit, so a missing --config/STAGEHAND_CONFIG is indistinguishable from 'discovery default absent', causing buildDeps to auto-detect+invoke a real installed agent.
2. INPUT: opts.ConfigPathOverride (string, from the --config flag via internal/cmd/root.go:79 PersistentPreRunE) and the STAGEHAND_CONFIG env var (already read in Load). loadTOML(path) -> (*Config, error) returning (nil,nil) on missing.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §15.2/§16.1 'overrides discovery' intent. In Load, BEFORE calling loadTOML, set `explicit := opts.ConfigPathOverride != "" || os.Getenv("STAGEHAND_CONFIG") != ""`. After `g, err := loadTOML(globalPath)`: if err != nil return wrapped 'global config' error (unchanged); if g == nil && explicit return fmt.Errorf("config file not found: %s", globalPath); else if g != nil overlay (unchanged). Do NOT modify loadTOML, file.go, or root.go. This is the ONLY production file touched.
4. OUTPUT: A Load() that returns a non-nil error for a missing explicit path; (nil) silently-absent discovery path unchanged. The error string contains 'config file not found' (the CLI wraps it as 'config: config file not found: <path>' -> exitcode.Error=1, before any provider resolution).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/cli.md §'Global flags' --config row (line ~20) and §'Config file paths' note in docs/configuration.md (line ~34) to state that an explicit --config / STAGEHAND_CONFIG pointing at a MISSING file fails fast with exit 1 (consistent with a malformed/directory path), rather than falling back to discovery. This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Issue 2 — Manifest-level output / strip_code_fence honored; [generation] becomes a true opt-in override (tri-state)
milestones.description: The bugfix-001 'Issue 4' bridge copies cfg.Output/cfg.StripCodeFence onto the resolved manifest UNCONDITIONALLY, but both fields carry non-empty/non-nil Defaults, so the [generation] default always wins and a manifest-level output='json' / strip_code_fence=false is silently clobbered (JSON mode dead at the manifest level; providers show lies about parsing). Fix: make Config.Output a *string (tri-state, mirroring the existing StripCodeFence *bool) and stop defaulting BOTH fields in Defaults(), so [generation] is opt-in and the manifest's own values (with Resolve() supplying the §12.1 raw/true fallbacks) win by default. PRD §12.1/§12.9/§12.4/§17.4.

tasks.title: Make the [generation] output/strip_code_fence override genuinely optional (tri-state) so manifest-level values win by default
tasks.description: Two coupled subtasks: (S1) change Config.Output from string to *string, stop defaulting Output AND StripCodeFence in Defaults(), and update the config-package loaders (materialize/overlay/loadGitConfig) + their tests; (S2) fix the buildDeps bridge in pkg/stagehand so 'if cfg.X != nil' means 'only override when [generation] explicitly set', then validate end-to-end that manifest-level output=json / strip_code_fence=false are honored and [generation] still overrides when set.

subtasks.title: Change Config.Output to *string and stop defaulting Output/StripCodeFence in Defaults(); update config-package loaders + tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/issue_analysis.md ISSUE 2 (blast radius verified SAFE + mechanical). Defaults() (internal/config/config.go:67-70) seeds Output:"raw" (string) and StripCodeFence:boolPtr(true) (*bool) — both non-nil/non-empty, defeating the buildDeps nil/empty guards. The manifest's own Resolve() (internal/provider/manifest.go:138-148, DefaultOutput/DefaultStripCodeFence) already supplies the §12.1 raw/true fallbacks, so Defaults() does NOT need to. loadEnv/loadFlags do NOT touch Output/StripCodeFence; cmd/config.go writes a static template (no Config marshal). The ONLY runtime consumer is buildDeps (the cfg->manifest copy) in S2.
2. INPUT: fileGeneration.Output is a plain string (internal/config/file.go:41) — KEEP it a string (go-toml decodes it fine); only the resolved Config.Output becomes *string. boolPtr helper exists at config.go:11.
3. LOGIC: (a) internal/config/config.go: change `Output string` -> `Output *string` (line ~35); add `func strPtr(s string) *string { return &s }` next to boolPtr; in Defaults() REMOVE the `Output:` and `StripCodeFence:` initializers (lines ~68-69) so both default to nil. (b) internal/config/file.go materialize (~159-160): `if g.Output != "" { o := g.Output; c.Output = &o }`; overlay (~210-211): `if src.Output != nil { dst.Output = src.Output }` (now pointer-symmetric with StripCodeFence; StripCodeFence lines 162-163/213-214 UNCHANGED). (c) internal/config/git.go loadGitConfig (~124-127): `if found { c.Output = &v }` (v is a per-loop local; &v safe); stripCodeFence (~152-155) UNCHANGED. Update tests to compile + reflect the new contract: config_test.go TestDefaults (39-44: assert Output==nil, StripCodeFence==nil post-Defaults); TestTOMLMarshalKeysAndNoColorExclusion (47-72: it marshals Defaults() and checks 'output ='/'strip_code_fence =' — with nil defaults those keys vanish; either marshal a Config with explicit strPtr/boolPtr values OR drop those two key assertions and keep the others); file_test.go (82-83 deref, 114 struct-literal strPtr("json"), 119-120 deref, 407 literal); git_test.go (111-112 deref, 133 `cfg.Output != nil`, 345-346 deref). Follow the compiler errors — every `cfg.Output != ""`/`Output: "..."` site is a compile break pointing at the next edit.
4. OUTPUT: A config package where Config.Output is *string and BOTH Output/StripCodeFence are nil in Defaults() (set only when a file/git-config layer explicitly provides them). `go build ./internal/config/... && go vet && go test ./internal/config/...` green. This is internal plumbing; the user-facing behavior is delivered in S2.
5. DOCS: none — internal type change with no user-facing/config surface change of its own (the [generation]/manifest semantics doc update rides with S2, which delivers the behavior).

subtasks.title: Fix the buildDeps bridge so [generation] output/strip_code_fence override the manifest only when explicitly set
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/issue_analysis.md ISSUE 2 (Part 2) + the end-to-end repro. buildDeps (pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go:206-211) currently does `if cfg.Output != ""` (becomes `!= nil` after S1) and `if cfg.StripCodeFence != nil`. After S1, cfg.Output/cfg.StripCodeFence are nil unless [generation]/git-config set them, so the guards become TRUE opt-in overrides. provider.ParseOutput (internal/provider/parse.go:48-58) reads the MANIFEST's *r.Output/*r.StripCodeFence — so leaving them unset lets the manifest's own output=json/strip_code_fence=false win (providers show will then be truthful). The existing bugfix-001 test TestGenerateCommit_ManifestOutputWins_WhenCfgOutputEmpty_Issue4 (pkg/stagehand/stagehand_test.go:~846) forced cfg.Output="" to fake this; under the fix it becomes the natural nil path.
2. INPUT: config.Config with Output *string / StripCodeFence *bool, both nil by default (from S1). The resolved provider.Manifest m (from reg.Get) carries its own [provider.X] output/strip_code_fence (already *string/*bool, possibly nil -> Resolve() fills raw/true).
3. LOGIC: In pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go buildDeps (~206-211) change to `if cfg.Output != nil { m.Output = cfg.Output }` (drop the local copy) and leave `if cfg.StripCodeFence != nil { m.StripCodeFence = cfg.StripCodeFence }` as-is. Both branches are now symmetric. Add end-to-end tests via the repo-local .stagehand.toml fixtures setupTestRepo/setupScriptedRepo (stagehand_test.go:60-180) exercising the REAL config.Load+registry path: (a) [provider.stub] output="json"+json_field+STAGEHAND_STUB_OUT='{"msg":"feat: x"}', NO [generation] -> GenerateCommit DryRun returns Message == parsed json_field (NOT the raw JSON blob); (b) [provider.stub] strip_code_fence=false with fenced stub output -> fence preserved in Message; (c) [generation] output="json" overrides a [provider.stub] output="raw" manifest -> JSON parsed; (d) regression: no [generation], manifest default raw -> raw still works. Update the bugfix-001 Issue-4 test comment/wording from 'cfg.Output is empty'/'!=""' to 'nil'/'!= nil' (cosmetic) and the forced `cfg.Output = ""` to `cfg.Output = nil`.
4. OUTPUT: pkg/stagehand.buildDeps honors manifest-level output/strip_code_fence by default and only overrides when [generation] explicitly sets them; providers show now matches actual parsing. `go test -race ./pkg/stagehand/...` green.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/configuration.md line ~84 ('These [generation] values override any per-provider defaults — the broader layer wins') to state output/strip_code_fence are an OPT-IN OVERRIDE: when [generation] omits them, the per-provider [provider.X] value is honored (raw/true remain the §12.1 manifest-level defaults). Also align docs/configuration.md §'Built-in defaults' (lines ~79-80) and the [generation] block comment (lines ~55-56). Update docs/providers.md line ~124 and the schema rows (lines ~30-32) with the same opt-in-override wording so providers show and parsing semantics agree.

milestones.title: Issue 3 — Unresolved merge conflicts surface a clean 'resolve merge conflicts first' message instead of raw multi-line git write-tree stderr
milestones.description: On unresolved conflicts, git.WriteTree returns a noisy multi-line error (f.txt: unmerged ×N + 'fatal: git-write-tree: error building trees') instead of PRD §13.5's clean single line. Behavior is already correct (exit 1, model never invoked, HEAD/index untouched — WriteTree is pipeline step 3, pre-generation); only the message is wrong. Localized fix to the WriteTree error path (covers both CommitStaged and runPipeline call sites). PRD §13.5/§18.2.

tasks.title: Return a clean single-line 'resolve merge conflicts' error from git.WriteTree on the unmerged-index failure
tasks.description: Modify internal/git/git.go WriteTree so that on failure (code != 0) it returns a single clean line naming the conflict (dropping the raw multi-line stderr), preferring an accurate ls-files -u check to distinguish 'unmerged paths' from other write-tree failures. The error propagates unchanged through both call sites (generate.CommitStaged step 3 and pkg/stagehand.runPipeline step 3) -> handleGenError default branch -> exitcode.Error (1) + 'stagehand: <clean msg>'. Still pre-generation, still exit 1, HEAD untouched.

subtasks.title: Clean the git.WriteTree unmerged-conflict error message (single line, no raw stderr)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/issue_analysis.md ISSUE 3. git.WriteTree (internal/git/git.go:218-224) on code != 0 returns fmt.Errorf("git write-tree: unresolved merge conflicts in index (exit %d): %s", code, strings.TrimSpace(stderr)) — the %s appends the FULL noisy multi-line git stderr. The error surfaces via handleGenError (internal/cmd/default_action.go:188 default branch) -> exitcode.New(Error, err) -> main prints 'stagehand: <msg>' + exit 1. Correct behavior; only the message is noisy. The existing test TestWriteTree_MergeConflict (internal/git/writetree_test.go:122-133) asserts strings.Contains(err.Error(), "unresolved merge conflicts") — KEEP that substring so the assertion still holds.
2. INPUT: g.run(ctx, workDir, "write-tree") -> (stdout, stderr, code, err). The run() helper is the ONLY git exec seam (git.go:95); an additional g.run(ctx, workDir, "ls-files", "-u") call is available to detect unmerged entries (non-empty stdout => conflicts).
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §13.5 'resolve merge conflicts first'. In WriteTree, when code != 0: PREFERRED — run `git ls-files -u`; if its stdout is non-empty, return a clean single-line error that KEEPS the substring 'unresolved merge conflicts' (e.g. errors.New("unresolved merge conflicts in the index — resolve them first, then re-run stagehand")); else return the original detailed error (a non-conflict write-tree failure). MINIMAL acceptable variant — drop the %s stderr and return a clean single line (exit-128 on a populated index is unambiguously unmerged). Either way: do NOT append the multi-line raw stderr to the conflict message. The extra ls-files call is on the FAILURE path only (not hot). Do NOT change the call sites (generate.go step 3 / stagehand.go runPipeline step 3) — they return the WriteTree error as-is.
4. OUTPUT: A WriteTree whose conflict error is a single clean line; exit code semantics and call sites unchanged. Update writetree_test.go: keep the existing 'unresolved merge conflicts' contains-assertion AND ADD an assertion that the message does NOT contain the raw noise ('fatal: git-write-tree', 'error building trees'). Reuse the makeMergeConflict fixture (writetree_test.go:22-68) as-is.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] If docs/how-it-works.md §'Failure modes and exit codes' (line ~53) quotes or describes the merge-conflict wording, align it to the clean single-line message; otherwise 'none — message-only change with no new config/API surface'.

milestones.title: Issue 4 — --dry-run generation failures get a dry-run-specific outcome (exit 1, short message, no rescue recipe)
milestones.description: Because --dry-run (correctly, per bugfix-001/FR49) runs the full pipeline incl. the snapshot, a generation failure surfaces the full §18.3 rescue block (Tree ID + manual commit-tree recipe) and exit 3/124 — surprising for a preview that was never going to commit. Fix is localized to the CLI layer (internal/cmd/default_action.go handleGenError): on dry-run + *RescueError, print a short stderr line and exit 1, omitting the recovery recipe. The public library API (pkg/stagehand) is UNCHANGED — it still returns *RescueError. PRD §9.12/FR49/§15.4.

tasks.title: Map dry-run generation failures (timeout/rescue) to exit 1 with a short message, omitting the rescue recovery recipe
tasks.description: In internal/cmd/default_action.go handleGenError, add a dry-run branch BEFORE the existing *RescueError branch: when flagDryRun is true and errors.As(err, &re *generate.RescueError) holds (covers BOTH ErrTimeout and ErrRescue), print a concise stderr message (no FormatRescue recovery recipe) and return exitcode.New(exitcode.Error, nil) (exit 1, silent). The library still returns *RescueError; only the CLI rendering special-cases dry-run. CAS/nothing-to-commit/generic errors on dry-run are unaffected.

subtasks.title: Add the dry-run RescueError branch in handleGenError (exit 1, short message, no recipe)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/issue_analysis.md ISSUE 4. handleGenError (internal/cmd/default_action.go:169-188) currently does: errors.As(err,&re) -> prints generate.FormatRescue(...) to stderr + exitcode.New(Rescue or Timeout, nil). flagDryRun is a package var in internal/cmd (root.go:40), readable directly from handleGenError (same package). pkg/stagehand.runPipeline returns a *generate.RescueError{Kind: ErrTimeout|ErrRescue} for dry-run failures (unchanged here). IMPORTANT regression guards: the COMMIT-path CLI tests TestRunDefault_Rescue (default_action_test.go:504 -> exit 3) and TestRunDefault_Timeout (default_action_test.go:563 -> exit 124) do NOT use --dry-run, so they STAY as-is. The LIBRARY test pkg/stagehand/stagehand_test.go TestGenerateCommit_Timeout 'dryrun' subtest (~296-362) asserts the library returns *RescueError{Kind:ErrTimeout} -> exitcode.For==Timeout(124); this STILL HOLDS because the library is unchanged — only the CLI wraps it to exit 1. Do NOT modify that library test.
2. INPUT: handleGenError(stderr io.Writer, err error) error, plus the package var flagDryRun. err is a *generate.RescueError (Kind is ErrTimeout or ErrRescue) on the dry-run failure path.
3. LOGIC: At the TOP of handleGenError (before the existing errors.As(err,&re) block), add: `if flagDryRun { var re *generate.RescueError; if errors.As(err, &re) { msg := "could not generate a commit message; run without --dry-run to see retries and the recovery recipe"; if errors.Is(err, generate.ErrTimeout) { msg = "generation timed out; run without --dry-run to see the recovery recipe" }; fmt.Fprintln(stderr, msg); return exitcode.New(exitcode.Error, nil) } }`. (exitcode.Error==1, silent -> main prints nothing extra.) Then the existing branches handle non-dry-run rescue/CAS/nothing/generic as before. Add CLI tests via rootCmd.SetArgs({...,"--dry-run"}) + setupStubRepoWithTimeout (default_action_test.go:109): (a) --dry-run + sleeping stub past timeout -> exitcode.For(err)==exitcode.Error (1) + short timeout message in stderr + NO 'git commit-tree' recovery recipe; (b) --dry-run + blank/unparseable stub (rescue) -> exit 1 + short message + no recipe. Model these on TestRunDefault_DryRun (268) and TestRunDefault_Timeout (563).
4. OUTPUT: handleGenError returns exitcode.Error (1) for dry-run generation failures with a concise stderr line and no rescue recipe; non-dry-run paths and the library contract unchanged. `go test -race ./internal/cmd/... ./pkg/stagehand/...` green.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/cli.md §'Exit codes' (line ~76) and the --dry-run flag row in §'Global flags' (line ~26) to note that a --dry-run GENERATION failure (timeout or parse/dedupe exhaustion) exits 1 with a short message rather than 3/124 + a recovery recipe (since no commit was ever intended).

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B — final sweep)
milestones.description: Per documentation-sync §5 Mode B: cross-cutting overview/feature docs that only make sense once the whole changeset is in place are swept here. README.md and the docs/how-it-works.md overview describe the config-override, generation-output, dry-run, and merge-conflict behaviors at a summary level and must reflect the four fixes. This task depends on EVERY implementing subtask so it runs last.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README.md + overview docs) to reflect all four bugfix-002 fixes
tasks.description: Sweep the top-level README.md and the docs/how-it-works.md architecture overview so the summarized behaviors match the implemented changes. Per-file docs touched directly by an implementing subtask (docs/cli.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/providers.md) were already updated as Mode A in their own subtasks — this task only reconciles the cross-cutting overview/feature blurbs.

subtasks.title: Update README.md to reflect the config-path, generation-output, and dry-run behavior changes
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/system_context.md + issue_analysis.md. README.md currently says (line ~119) the [generation] section output/strip_code_fence 'tune how Stagehand parses agent output across all providers (overriding per-provider values)' and (line ~121) '--config ... is honored by every command'; the Quick start shows `stagehand --dry-run` as a preview (line ~66). After the fixes: an explicit --config/STAGEHAND_CONFIG to a MISSING file errors (Issue 1); [generation] output/strip_code_fence is an OPT-IN override and per-provider manifest values are honored when [generation] omits them (Issue 2); --dry-run generation failures exit 1 with a short message, not 3/124 (Issue 4).
2. INPUT: The implemented behavior from P1.M1.T1.S1 (missing explicit config -> exit 1), P1.M2.T1.S1/S2 (manifest output/strip honored; [generation] opt-in), P1.M4.T1.S1 (dry-run failure -> exit 1).
3. LOGIC: Reconcile README.md with the implemented behavior WITHOUT over-claiming. (a) §'Configure your agent' (~119): reword 'overriding per-provider values' to reflect that [generation] output/strip_code_fence OVERRIDE per-provider values ONLY when set; otherwise the per-provider manifest value is used. (b) The --config note (~121): if it implies a missing file silently falls back, clarify it must point at an existing file or Stagehand errors. (c) §'Quick start' / FAQ (~66, ~189+): if dry-run is described as a safe preview, optionally note a generation failure exits 1 (keep concise — do not bloat the README). Do NOT duplicate the per-file reference docs (docs/cli.md/configuration.md/providers.md were updated in Mode A subtasks). Keep changes minimal and accurate.
4. OUTPUT: README.md accurately summarizes the four fixes; no stale 'broader layer wins' / silent-fallback claims remain.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B final sweep). No further doc subtask is created.

subtasks.title: Reconcile docs/how-it-works.md overview (failure/exit-code + rescue sections) with the merge-conflict and dry-run fixes
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bugfix/002_18158df10968/architecture/issue_analysis.md ISSUES 3 & 4. docs/how-it-works.md §'Failure modes and exit codes' (~53) and §'Rescue protocol' (~66) describe the snapshot/rescue/exit-code story. Issue 3 changed the merge-conflict message to a clean single line (still exit 1, pre-generation); Issue 4 made --dry-run generation failures exit 1 with a short message instead of 3/124 + a recovery recipe.
2. INPUT: Implemented behavior from P1.M3.T1.S1 (clean merge-conflict message) and P1.M4.T1.S1 (dry-run failure -> exit 1, no rescue recipe).
3. LOGIC: If docs/how-it-works.md §'Failure modes and exit codes' or §'Rescue protocol' describes the merge-conflict wording or implies --dry-run can exit 3/124 with a rescue recipe, reconcile it: note that merge conflicts produce a clean 'resolve merge conflicts first' message (exit 1), and that --dry-run generation failures exit 1 with a short message (the full rescue recipe is reserved for real commits). Keep changes accurate and minimal; if a section already matches, leave it. Do NOT touch docs/cli.md/configuration.md/providers.md (already Mode-A updated).
4. OUTPUT: docs/how-it-works.md overview is consistent with the implemented merge-conflict and dry-run behavior.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B final sweep). No further doc subtask is created.
